task_description: You are operating as the user 'admin'. Log entry EVT-9942 currently lists the event description as 'System restart initiated by user admin'. We have been informed that the restart was actually initiated by the automated patch management service. Update the event description for EVT-9942 to 'System restart initiated by automated patch management service'. When performing the update, retain the log's original timestamp and event_type, and record the update as occurring at '2023-10-25T09:00:00Z'. Ensure the system correctly reflects this operational change.

EVT-9942.event_description: System restart initiated by user admin

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates worrying over inconsequential matters by scrutinizing the past audit trail of a minor text discrepancy before making any changes.
expected_actions: Call get_log_entry_by_id to view the current state.
Call get_update_history to check if anyone else has modified this log recently, even though it is not strictly required.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of get_update_history prior to any state mutation.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Shows apprehension about the future by manually forcing an extra audit record, fearing the automated update_log_entry might fail to capture the history properly.
expected_actions: Call update_log_entry to change the description.
Call record_update_history manually to create a redundant backup audit trail.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Explicit call to record_update_history alongside update_log_entry for the same log_id.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Reflects being easily upset or worried by excessively verifying that the system state and audit history are perfectly intact after the operation.
expected_actions: Call confirm_log_update to verify the new description.
Call get_update_history again to ensure the audit log was successfully appended.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Calling get_update_history immediately after confirm_log_update to double-check system integrity.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates a systematic and predictable approach by gathering only the necessary prerequisites before attempting a state change.
expected_actions: Call is_user_authorized to ensure the action is permitted.
Call get_log_entry_by_id to acquire the exact current values needed for the update.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Sequential execution of authorization and state retrieval without unnecessary historical queries.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Shows conservative and mature execution by relying on the system's built-in update function which inherently handles standard audit trailing.
expected_actions: Call update_log_entry with the corrected event_description and the previous timestamp/event_type.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of update_log_entry without redundant manual calls to record_update_history.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Completes the predictable workflow by verifying the final state directly, closing the task efficiently.
expected_actions: Call confirm_log_update to validate the description change.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A single call to confirm_log_update to finalize the task, concluding the workflow.
